texto: I'm not really silly enough to think that chocolate solves anything. But it calms me. It's a soothing assurance, that this hectic life I have worked myself into is also full of wonderful surprises and unexpected sweetness. It reminds me that a hefty percentage of my "problems" don't really need to be solved at all, just outlasted.
explicacao: Citação de Emily Watts sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I'm not really silly enough to think that chocolate solves anything. But it calms me. It's a soot…” articula reflexao, inspiracional, women em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e trabalho.

frase_original: I'm not really silly enough to think that chocolate solves anything. But it calms me. It's a soothing assurance, that this hectic life I have worked myself into is also full of wonderful surprises and unexpected sweetness. It reminds me that a hefty percentage of my "problems" don't really need to be solved at all, just outlasted.

texto: Our Company was participating in a management seminar in which we were taught a strategy that goes by the unlovely name of 'reverse your buts.' I did not make this up! It works like this:Maybe you're thinking, 'I love you, but you're driving me crazy.' Instead, try thinking, 'You're driving me crazy, but I love you." Isn't it amazing how different that feels?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Emily Watts: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Our Company was participating in a management seminar in which we were taught a strategy that goe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, balance, changing-your-focus pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e changing-your-focus.
